learning_outcomes.description: Describe form, function and coordination of key physiological processes.
Compare and contrast biological strategies across varied organisms that reflect adaptation.
Identify and describe interactions within and between organisms, and the environment.
Conduct and analyse data from laboratory studies to test hypotheses.

assessments.due_date:
Set 1 of 9: Week 2 Mon - Week 2 Tue

Set 2 of 9: Week 4 Mon - Week 4 Tue

Set 3 of 9: Week 5 Mon - Week 5 Tue

Set 4 of 9: Week 6 Mon - Week 6 Tue

Set 5 of 9: Week 7 Mon - Week 7 Tue

Set 6 of 9: Week 8 Mon - Week 8 Tue

Set 7 of 9: Week 9 Mon - Week 9 Tue

Set 9 of 9: Week 12 Mon - Week 12 Tue

Task sets will take place during or immediately after specific PRA or WKS.
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Quiz
assessments.task_description:
Nine of the PRA/WKS activities have associated assessment task sets. Each set may include a variety of competency-based, analytical, and/or comprehension tasks that you will need to demonstrate or respond to, during or immediately after the PRA or WKS. Your best six of the nine task sets will be worth 5% each and automatically counted towards 30% of your course grade (cumulative).

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will complete these task sets during or immediately after specific PRA or WKS. Competency-based tasks may require you to demonstrate a practical technique or measurement skill that gets assessed by your tutor during a PRA, while analytical or comprehension tasks may require you to verbally describe or provide a written response to questions, and assessed by your lecturer or tutor during or immediately after PRA or WKS.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The in-semester exam will cover all material (online, APP, PRA, WKS activities) from Weeks 1 to 6.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The end-of-semester exam will cover all material (online, APP, PRA, WKS activities) from Weeks 7 to 13. It will not cover material from Weeks 1 to 6.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
